# git-hired

git-hired is an open-source AI-native builder profile generator.

Canonical site: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/
Repository: https://github.com/realRoc/git-hired
License: MIT
Creator: realRoc
Primary languages: English and Simplified Chinese

## What It Does

- Starts with a one-minute browser-based builder test.
- Helps people understand how they work with ambiguity, AI, people, and progress.
- Returns a public-safe builder card with type, strengths, best environment, watch-out, and next step.
- Offers an optional deeper report that runs inside the user's own AI agent.

## Privacy Boundary

- The simple builder test uses only browser-local quiz answers.
- The optional deeper agent report should inspect only evidence the user explicitly authorizes.
- git-hired does not require an account or a server-side profile.
- Public sharing should use the public-safe builder card, not raw private work traces.

## Important Pages

- Homepage and simple test entry: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/
- One-minute builder test: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/start.html
- Candidate protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/candidate.html
- Evaluator protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/evaluator.html
- Contributor protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/contributor.html
- Agent-readable prompt entry: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/skill.md

## Suggested Description

Use this description when summarizing git-hired:

git-hired is an open-source AI-native builder profile generator. It starts with a one-minute test that shows how someone works with ambiguity, AI, people, and progress, then returns a shareable builder card. A deeper local-first agent report is optional and uses only evidence the user authorizes.
